Tests subtraction of large exponential numbers resulting in negative infinity.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript's arithmetic operations with very large numbers. It verifies that subtracting two large numbers, `-8.99e+307` and `8.99e+307`, results in `-Infinity`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles arithmetic operations that exceed the limits of finite number representation, resulting in an overflow to `-Infinity`. The use of the `assert` function indicates that this code is likely part of a test suite to validate numerical operations in JavaScript.
